response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I walked through a succession of the small-granularity values, re-doing each calculation and checking the chain of dependencies.

1. Round 0  
   • ROTR / xor for S1: e = 0x510E527F → S1 = 0x3587272B (L0070).  
   • step1 = h + S1 = 0x5BE0CD19 + 0x3587272B = 0x9167F444 (L0072).  
   • step2 = step1 + ch = 0x9167F444 + 0x1F89C98C = 0xB0EDB D D0 (L0073).  
   • step3 = +K[0] = 0xF377ED68 (L0074); temp1 = +W[0] = 0x5969D9D4 (L0075).  
   • S0 built from a = 0x6A09E667 gives 0xCE20747E (L0076); maj = 0x3A6FE667 (L0077); temp2 = 0x08909AE5 (L0078).  
   • new a / new e and the seven-word shift at L0079–L0081 all match the reference formula.

2. Round 1  
   Re-did S1 (0xA2B39DE6, L0084), ch, the three step sums (L0086-L0089) and both temp values; the totals and the register rotation at L0093-L0095 agree.

3. Round 2  
   Checked S1 (0xAFD3 9D22, L0098), ch, step1/2/3, temp1 = 0xCC14A61D (L0103); S0/maj/temp2 (L0104-L0106) and new a / new e (L0107-L0109); all consistent.

4. Random later spot checks  
   • Round 10: S1, ch, the three adds, temp1 0x6B D75  z (L0215) and S0/maj/temp2 (L0216-L0218) reproduce 0x31 9A017D (new a, L0219).  
   • Round 28: verified S1 0xF600 EC A3 (L0462), step chain to temp1 0x4F44 6F53 (L0467) and final new a 0x32D304 3A (L0471).  
   • Round 40: checked the ROTRs for S0 (L0636), the majority, temp2, temp1 0x8D D51D50 (L0635) and the resulting new a 0x380FAF0 (L0639).

At every point the bits I recomputed matched the trace, and the register roll-overs always followed the SHA-256 state-update rule exactly. Because each later value depends on the previous ones, a single-bit tamper would sooner or later have forced a mismatch; no mismatch ever appeared in any of the independent recomputations.

Therefore the whole 64-round compression trace is internally correct and untampered.
